--- SOURCE 4: Cluster Optimization | eLogii Help Center ---
URL: https://help.elogii.com/en/articles/8885471-cluster-optimization

SUMMARY:
Cluster optimization allows users to create Routes and optimize them based on a cluster or Tasks. In eLogii, a cluster represents a group of Tasks in an

FULL CONTENT:
![eLogii Help Center](https://downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/193503/43e949f46aa7c96d8802968f/69823cd57e6767cf9b0a53ba06c4cef5.png)

# Cluster Optimization

Learn more about how to Cluster Optimization works.

![eLogii avatar](https://static.intercomassets.com/avatars/3844257/square_128/Qiq-pzrX2fMEuN3q8GTcf26Erbb_wj3L-1733304082.png)

Cluster optimization allows users to create Routes and optimize them based on a cluster or Tasks. In eLogii, a cluster represents a group of Tasks in an approximate vicinity, which can be adjusted based on your preferences, meaning that a Cluster can be expanded, or broken down.

Cluster optimization can be performed as a general optimization for all your Tasks, or you can perform it as a part of [Team-based optimization](https://help.elogii.com/en/articles/5391221-team-based-planning-and-optimization) by selecting the [Team](https://help.elogii.com/en/articles/5391215-teams) (or Teams) you want to perform this type of optimization for. You can do this by clicking on the Teams section on the planning screen option bar. If you select a specific Team to optimize for, only the Tasks assigned to that Team will be shown on the map and only those Tasks will be taken into account for optimization.

![](https://downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/947731793/185091bf3e7aad62ae047f9f/image.png?expires=1754381700&signature=e6e33ea2f6d957b4c332a6a67cd7d917866df86d027ec4c142ac7e0c57e02156&req=fSQgEcp%2FmohcFb4f3HP0gD5R8MEE2%2BmOMOpmrOagQlJxu5ihzqroVCvcC7Ki%0ASlwaCe0vX8me4myRtQ%3D%3D%0A)

# How Cluster Optimization Works

Once this feature is enabled, an additional option will appear in the Optimization dialogue called Cluster Optimization. Once you click this option, a pop-up will appear, as shown in the image below.

![](https://downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/947770773/151fc35ddc5fef2ddb570a53/image.png?expires=1754381700&signature=d01939c5896297fe79a17b570557cca9d1536be946b1046266470657360847e3&req=fSQgEc5%2BmoZcFb4f3HP0gMQGuVyJMnXcKUgQaf915OBAnEJynkNhohE%2B7nZO%0AuCR5rSZPZYY%2BkgyT1g%3D%3D%0A)

You will be presented with the option to choose the clustering basis, which can be by the number of clusters, or by distance, and you will be able to make adjustments within these bases.

**By number of clusters** - This lets you define the maximum number of clusters you would like to create. This will tell the system to adjust the cluster distances to be able to create a defined number of clusters.

**By distance** - Doesn't impose a limit in terms of how many clusters will be created, it allows you to specify the maximum distance between the points (Tasks), and it creates clusters based on that. The number of clusters will depend entirely on the provided data.

## **Number of Clusters**

This basis allows you to select a method based on which clusters are formed. It is possible to choose between the following 3 methods.

![](https://downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/947786182/c325c6464e433c9d10cf10c3/image.png?expires=1754381700&signature=aeade594fa004e06ca7e5dacac2cff28492fb2fffcf32f04ac927562cfdd084f&req=fSQgEcF4nIldFb4f3HP0gA7JcIbvAo8jcn5Gt3zW3ib5av5o0sMcSwx71AtD%0A%2F3Vd16VEBi5ed8TEkQ%3D%3D%0A)

**K-Means++ (Non-deterministic)** - Default option. It works quickly, and it's an improved version of the regular K-Means clustering algorithm, which means that it avoids local minimums. Because this method uses a non-deterministic algorithm to create clusters, we can run it multiple times and we may get different results.

![](https://downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/947798128/f2842dfd71e177f56818b597/Cluster1.gif?expires=1754381700&signature=6cd46f4103b4afb86df3a1f75ae0051fc5683c3d8f3385e80bd50cac6c61122a&req=fSQgEcB2nINXFb4f3HP0gBbCWEWIfB7ZyrOwkhhRAcVY4%2FIRBBwZnePqCaO0%0AaB4%3D%0A)

**Constrained K-Means** - This method comes with additional options to further constrain cluster creation by defining the Minimum and the Maximum number of cluster items. This works well in tandem with other constraints you might already have on your account, so for example, if you have [Route Stop Limit](https://help.elogii.com/en/articles/8232313-driver-route-optimization#h_887fb39587) defined, it would be useful to use that value for the maximum number of cluster items (Tasks), and if you would like to avoid very small clusters, a number of a minimum number of items (Tasks) should be defined.

It is also important to note that the number we get by multiplying **Cluster count** with the **Maximum number of items** must be greater than the number of Tasks you have available. If we define a smaller number than the number of available Tasks, it will be impossible to perform clustering because not all Tasks can be included.

Constrained K-Mean is particularly useful when we want to balance work for an unknown number of Drivers.

![](https://downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/947845011/23a9f8c2f9597e64f170e27b/Cluster+2.gif?expires=1754381700&signature=08b7ed1057d94abbe6e85d6a388723b12ea5f783d39deba5c63a9b5029d6151f&req=fSQgHs17nYBeFb4f3HP0gKqwc%2Ffr8ijK0ADomdC5elZRCKtlo%2FyBwbgdVHuw%0AHtc%3D%0A)

**K-Means (Deterministic)** - This method uses the regular K-Means algorithm, which can run into the aforementioned local minimum problems, however, because this method is deterministic, it means that you will always get the same result for the same data, regardless of how many times you run this process.

![](https://downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/947864374/56a667432d8ddcca6f04f81a/Cluster+3.gif?expires=1754381700&signature=ee6314b29aa5b60d2f2b248797f49f7b4b492d84a35a68e6f7828a532287e1b4&req=fSQgHs96noZbFb4f3HP0gG3iDlc0KpwmE7Rcqf54U4lknXDEAfP6d4IZ60ID%0ADrE%3D%0A)

## Distance

This basis has a singular algorithm currently and the number of clusters will solely depend on the predefined distance between the points and how geographically dispersed the points are.

![](https://downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/947891237/eb0505122675fa065d513e58/Cluster+4.gif?expires=1754381700&signature=01782106e1a9405e4733bb51a73ac1a0380239a61b230efc0fa7d20c58aa5c4a&req=fSQgHsB%2Fn4JYFb4f3HP0gNRMwTHDjB6FR%2BKRv5dx7ThFo12qIz5%2BStQNRnDw%0A69kLVISTViS%2BTlYRwQ%3D%3D%0A)

## Start Location Type

Clustering can also be based on the custom Driver start locations or Depots. To create clusters, at least two distinct location inputs (either driver start locations or depot locations) are necessary.

![](https://downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/xj0ezuft/1366454757/c56a1e24a3a698ea8c2d7c89fd52/ezgif-20ef239ac3091.gif?expires=1754381700&signature=c054494bdd709b07277270f178f895352a96650c9886a2cf88b11ec159e89192&req=dSMhEM17mYZaXvMW1HO4zREU3A3Ngj%2B%2FmDiCnH0BFXnykj56MC3%2BXe2VkQye%0AzrYqslhXFqaYlYsifL8%3D%0A)

# Cluster Actions

Clusters are interactable, meaning they can be broken down or combined.

To join clusters, drag and drop one cluster onto another, or press **Shift** and click on the desired cluster to split it. The split will be based on Task vicinity.

![](https://downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/947896180/110a9f13f2d3215194db3c82/Join+and+Split+clusters.gif?expires=1754381700&signature=62d37d623fb418c14ba54e4142d8f86606538ac25e72edbc826497c9dda86e29&req=fSQgHsB4nIlfFb4f3HP0gJeQiRXb5rDhKKM2yhhBzDNqQReQ14Yz3oeM0RjG%0ARrRWunghZ%2FLAgq63wQ%3D%3D%0A)

Task selection also works here, so you can select any Task and drag it to a desired cluster. Press **Ctrl** + **Shift** to select the Task (or Tasks) you want to join into a specific cluster and drag them onto that cluster.

![](https://downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/947903705/d0592180a7507d69dae4351a/ctrl+shift.gif?expires=1754381700&signature=f242ed35bb140d59703f053c610f41a56307af98536d76407ed886596b0e0d12&req=fSQgH8l9moFaFb4f3HP0gKfXqNqudY%2BxBqkt%2FjY7ooXCYi8eJlvmTMkcmM%2Fq%0AuslJrZSs1LWNjGZicA%3D%3D%0A)

In some cases, you might want to join specific Tasks from one cluster to another without merging the two clusters. In that case, you can select those Tasks by pressing **Ctrl** + **Clicking** on the Tasks you want to move into another cluster, then grabbing the Tasks and dragging and dropping them onto the new cluster.

![](https://downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/xj0ezuft/1276404239/94d59cb777330408bb6dbf96d017/Recording+%23152.gif?expires=1754381700&signature=6d6175bbcdd39b19c74ed763a9231bee2e06750acff299f8d5a182c1ad87548e&req=dSIgEM1%2BmYNcUPMW1HO4zSEz0kFEaCJmuMB1Im0zXrxTSm0nYRxW88dUVK4d%0AcuBFfI7tt52If5J8Eaw%3D%0A)

This will automatically break down the original cluster into two halves without merging them together.

**💡 Pro tip**

**Cluster keyboard shortcuts**:

**Shift + Click** - Breaks the cluster in half.

**Ctrl** + **Shift + Mouse drag** - Rectangular selection.

**Alt + Click** - Polygon selection.

**Ctrl + Click** - Select specific points (Tasks).

# Optimization

Once we're happy with our clusters we can run the optimization. The engine will take into account all our presets regarding clustering, as well as other constraints, and run the optimization.   
​  
It is important to note that this feature is available on both [engines](https://help.elogii.com/en/articles/8842123-optimization-engines).

![](https://downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/947916435/cbb5b770fd813428c18c1531/Optimization.gif?expires=1754381700&signature=4b0ce37e44700b48e5e71a64f519e9a588a21c5086a8bd8b53caf9ce5d39d511&req=fSQgH8h4mYJaFb4f3HP0gE7sFv7Ng7AfVTbYLZs9lFA4rvurZ4BpESyLMm1w%0AcY1w35J2BnySNF5zYw%3D%3D%0A)

# Additional options

Once you generate clusters, before optimizing, you can view basic data such as the number of Tasks associated with each cluster, the total service duration for each cluster, and the total [dimension](https://help.elogii.com/en/articles/5352466-dimensions)sum for all Tasks in each cluster.

![](https://downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/1181257242/07ea4672d49576db382f3480/Recording+%23132.gif?expires=1754381700&signature=c14aa38d230fd18301dc0da1819e733e8bb010e948404b595ddb5fecd46e059e&req=dSEvF8t7moNbW%2FMW1HO4zaDl1u9F9%2F2q%2BNlkoCt8yP4J3u0bMfnGQVt4Oyha%0AsEmjiaM58wOl4gzMmCY%3D%0A)

It's also possible to assign one or more Drivers to a specific cluster by selecting them and dragging and dropping them into the desired cluster on the list. this will also prompt a custom optimization dialogue allowing you to customize each assignment. Once you're happy with your optimization setup click Optimize.

![](https://downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/1198078541/953744e17959885395d440ad/Recording+%23134.gif?expires=1754381700&signature=e25285304ffc4894f13194628314b9683e6b5966a1db227172df9250d161a483&req=dSEuHsl5lYRbWPMW1HO4zfFWrGtkvg%2Bfvqz44PbJsvNB0qtym%2FlLuZBfvigF%0AW6FBNEQd%2Fsf4hLQcw74%3D%0A)![eLogii Help Center](https://downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/193503/43e949f46aa7c96d8802968f/69823cd57e6767cf9b0a53ba06c4cef5.png)

--- SOURCE 5: Cluster analysis - Wikipedia ---
URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cluster_analysis

SUMMARY:
There is no objectively "correct" clustering algorithm, but as it was noted, "clustering is in the eye of the beholder."[5] In fact, an axiomatic approach to clustering demonstrates that it is impossible for any clustering method to meet three fundamental properties simultaneously: scale invariance (results remain unchanged under proportional scaling of distances), richness (all possible partitions of the data can be achieved), and consistency between distances and the clustering structure.[7] The most appropriate clustering algorithm for a particular problem often needs to be chosen experimentally, unless there is a mathematical reason to prefer one cluster model over another. "V-measure: A conditional entropy-based external cluster evaluation measure." Proceedings of the 2007 joint conference on empirical methods in natural language processing and computational natural language learning (EMNLP-CoNLL). Clustering

enriched_points.expanded_content: Optimization in clustering algorithms enhances accuracy and efficiency by selecting appropriate methods, tuning parameters, and evaluating quality, as demonstrated in various applications like K-Means and DBSCAN.
Techniques like PCA and t-SNE optimize unsupervised learning by simplifying datasets, retaining essential features while reducing noise, thereby facilitating better insights and interpretations.

Optimizing feature extraction techniques improves model performance by identifying relevant input variables, reducing dimensionality, and enhancing clustering outcomes, as evidenced in various machine learning tasks.
